name: Chicken and Andouille Gumbo
recipeIngredient: 1 each Chicken; cut up or boned
recipeInstructions:
1. Season and brown chicken in oil (lard, bacon drippings) over medium-high heat.
2. Add sausage (cut into 1/4 inch rounds) to the pot and sauté with chicken. Remove both from the pot.
3. Make a roux using equal parts of oil and flour until desired color is reached (a dark chestnut color).
4. Add onions, celery, green pepper, and garlic to the roux, stirring continuously until vegetables reach desired tenderness.
5. Return chicken and sausage to the pot and cook with vegetables, stirring frequently.
6. Gradually stir in liquid and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to simmer and cook for an hour or more.
7. Season to taste.
8. Approximately 10 minutes before serving, add green onions.
9. Gumbo may be served over rice. Adding sherry at the table is optional. File (a fine green powder made from young dried and ground sassafras leaves) can be added by individuals if desired. Recommended amount: 1/4 to 1/2 tsp per serving.
10. If making roux over very high heat, ensure the oil used is free of food particles to avoid burning. If scorched, discard the roux and start over after cleaning the pot thoroughly.
11. For okra gumbo, add 4-6 cups of chopped okra to the trinity (onions, celery, green pepper), sauté, and cook together with the roux.
